Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 03.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Warehouse Number*
Number that identifies a complex, physical warehouse structure within the Warehouse Management system.
Type: Text Length: 80

WM Unit (ISO Format)
Unit of measure in which warehouse management manages the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Proposed UoM from Mat
Unit of measure to be used for this product in the warehouse.
Type: Text Length: 80

Capacity Usage
Using this neutral capacity usage indicator for a product, you can execute a capacity check in the Warehouse Management system (set for storage type level). You must make an entry for each procurement unit of measure.
Type: Number Length: 11 Decimal: 3

UoM for Capacity Usage (ISO Format)
The procurement unit of measure indicates to which product unit of measure the consumption usage refers.
Type: Text Length: 80

Picking Storage Type
Storage type used in rough-cut planning and detailed planning.
The picking storage type is of no significance for the creation of transfer orders.
Type: Text Length: 80

Stock Removal
Indicator determining the storage types selected by the system when removing stock and the priority with which they are accessed. You can predefine up to ten storage types in hierarchical order.
Type: Text Length: 80

Stock Replacement
Key that determines the storage types to be selected by the system and the priority in which they are accessed for stock placements. You can predefine up to ten storage types when defining strategies for storage type searches.
Type: Text Length: 80

Storage Section Ind.
Key used by the system to determine that a product is to be placed into one particular storage section in preference to another. It is possible to define up to ten storage sections in priority order when defining strategies for storage section searches.
Type: Text Length: 80

Bulk Storage
The bulk storage indicator classifies products according to the way they are stored in a bulk storage area. It describes how the products are to be stacked. This indicator is a decisive factor in the definition of the structure of a block/row within a bulk storage area.
Type: Text Length: 80

Special Movement
This indicator is used to separate special posting procedures for Products Management documents from the standard processing method. You can enter it manually for each goods movement in the Product Management system. The system uses it to determine the movement type in the Warehouse Management system. In this way, different movement types in the Warehouse Management system as well as different interim storage types can be assigned to one movement type in the Inventory Management system.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Message to Invent. Management
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Two-Step Picking
Key that determines whether 2-step picking will be used or not for a product that is designated for 2-step picking in the product master record.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Allow Addition to Stock
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Loading Equipment Quantity 1
In conjunction with the storage unit type, the system uses the loading equipment quantity to propose how to pack a quantity onto loading devices when placing a product into stock.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

UoM for LE Quantity 1 (ISO Format)
Unit of measure referring to the loading equipment quantity.
Type: Text Length: 80

1st Storage Unit Type
Key that can be used to classify storage units.
Type: Text Length: 80

Loading Equipment Quantity 2
In conjunction with the storage unit type, the system uses the loading equipment quantity to propose how to pack a quantity onto loading devices when placing a product into stock.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

UoM for LE Quantity 2 (ISO Format)
Unit of measure referring to the loading equipment quantity.
Type: Text Length: 80

2nd Storage Unit Type
Key that can be used to classify storage units.
Type: Text Length: 80

Loading Equipment Quantity 3
In conjunction with the storage unit type, the system uses the loading equipment quantity to propose how to pack a quantity onto loading devices when placing a product into stock.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

UoM for LE Quantity 3 (ISO Format)
Unit of measure referring to the loading equipment quantity.
Type: Text Length: 80

3rd Storage Unit Type
Key that can be used to classify storage units.
Type: Text Length: 80
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 03.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Warehouse Number*
Number that identifies a complex, physical warehouse structure within the Warehouse Management system.
Type: Text Length: 80

Storage Type*
Specifies a subdivision of a complex, physical warehouse. Different storage types are identified by their warehousing technique, form of organization, or their function.
Type: Text Length: 80

Storage Bin
Specifies the smallest addressable unit in a warehouse that is sometimes referred to as a slot. A storage bin identifies the exact location in the warehouse where goods can be stored. Note that the storage bin must already exist.
Type: Text Length: 80

Maximum Bin Quantity
Largest quantity that can be stored in a storage bin of this storage type. This quantity is recorded in the base quantity unit. This entry is used for checking storage bin capacity.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Minimum Bin Quantity
Minimum quantity which is stored in the bin location of this storage type. Quantity is recorded in the base quantity unit. The system can use this entry for a product replenishment in this bin location.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Control Quantity
Threshold value according to which the system decides whether or not it should propose a stock removal from this storage type for the quantity- dependent stock removal strategy or the large/small quantity picking strategy. This quantity is managed in the base unit of measure.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Replenishment Quantity
Quantity of product to be replenished in the storage bin. This quantity is managed in the base unit of measure.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3

Picking Area
Groups storage bins together from the standpoint of picking strategies and is a symmetrical counterpart to the storage section, which groups bins from the standpoint of putaway strategies. The picking area is therefore parallel in nature to the storage section and is situated hierarchically between the storage type and storage bin.
Type: Text Length: 80

Rounding Quantity
When using the quantity-dependent picking strategy (large/small quantities), the requested quantity is rounded down to this quantity. This quantity is managed in the base unit of measure.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 3
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 03.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Area*
Valuation area is the organizational level at which the product is valuated.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Type
This is a further subdivision of the valuation category. The valuation category defines the valuation types allowed for a product. If a product is valuated by origin (for example, valuation category H), you can define the possible countries of origin as valuation types. For valuation category 'Single batch Valuation' you have to provide for each batch an own valuation type with the detailed price information. Note: you have to define one valuation header record with specified valuation category and initial valuation type and one dependent record for each valuation type. Further information can be found in the migration object documentation under Mapping instructions - Single Batch Valuation and Split Valuation.
Type: Text Length: 10

Valuation Category
Determines whether stocks of the material are valuated together or separately. In the case of split valuation, this indicator also determines which valuation types are allowed, that is, the criteria by which stocks can be valuated. Further information can be found the migration object documentation under Mapping instructions - Single Batch Valuation and Split Valuation.
Type: Text Length: 80

Price Control Determination
The Price determination indicator determines whether price determination is: •Transaction-based •Single-level / multilevel
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Class*
Assignment of a product to a group of G/L accounts
With other factors, the valuation class determines the G/L accounts that are updated as a result of a valuation-relevant transaction or event such as a goods movement. The valuation class makes it possible to: •Post the stock values of products of the same product type to different G/L accounts •Post the stock values of products of different product types to the same G/L account
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Class for Sales Order
Default value for the valuation class for valuated sales order stocks of the product. You need specify a valuation class here only if you want to assign a different valuation class to this type of stock than that assigned to other valuated stocks of the product. This makes it possible to define other allowed G/L accounts for these stocks.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Class for Project Stock
Default value for the valuation class for valuated project stocks of the product. You need specify a valuation class here only if you want to assign a different valuation class to this type of stock than that assigned to other valuated stocks of the product. This makes it possible to define other allowed G/L accounts for these stocks.
Type: Text Length: 80

Price Control*
Indicates the price control used to valuate the stock of a product. You have the following options: • S - Standard price • V - Moving average price
Type: Text Length: 80

Currency*
You must specify the company code currency belonging to the valuation area.
Type: Text Length: 80

Inventory Price Moving Average
Moving average price if the product has price control indicator 'V' for moving average price. The system calculates the moving average price automatically by dividing the product value in the stock account by the total of all storage location stocks in the plant concerned. It changes the price with each valuation-relevant movement.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Inventory Price Standard Price
Standard price of the product if the product has price control indicator 'S' for standard price. The valuation of product stocks at standard prices means that all goods movements are valuated at the same price over an extended period.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Price Unit
Number of units to which the price refers.
Type: Number Length: 5

Future Price
Price at which the product will be valuated as from a precise date in the future. The future price is valid as of the date you enter in the relevant field.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Date of Which Future Price Is Valid From
Type: Date

Planned Price 1
Price that the system can take as a basis for product costing.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Planned Price Date 1
Date as from which planned price 1 is valid for the product.
Type: Date

Planned Price 2
Price that the system can take as a basis for product costing.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Planned Price Date 2
Date as from which planned price 2 is valid for the product.
Type: Date

Planned Price 3
Price that the system can take as a basis for product costing.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Planned Price Date 3
Date as from which planned price 3 is valid for the product.
Type: Date

Tax Price 1
Price at which the product is valuated for tax purposes.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Tax Price 2
Price at which the product is valuated for tax purposes, and which is derived in the determination of lowest value. It relates to the price unit of the previous year.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Tax Price 3
Price at which the product is valuated for reasons of tax law.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Price Unit
Number of units to which the price refers.
Type: Number Length: 5

Commercial Price 1
Price at which the product is valuated for commercial purposes.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Commercial Price 2
Price at which the product is valuated from the commercial viewpoint, and which is derived in the determination of lowest value.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Commercial Price 3
Price at which the product is valuated for reasons of commercial law. This price is given by the FIFO valuation.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

LIFO/FIFO Relevant
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 80

LIFO Pool
Alphanumeric key uniquely identifying the document. Products to be valuated together are grouped under a pool number.
Type: Text Length: 80

Devaluation Indicator
A devaluation indicator representing the number of years a product has already been classed as "slow or non-moving".
Type: Number Length: 2

Usage of the Product
Use for BRAZIL only !
This indicator shows the intended usage of a particular product in inventory. Please note that each usage of a product must have a separate valuation. As soon as a product has more than one usage in a valuation area then valuation types can be used.
Type: Text Length: 80

Origin of the Product
Use for BRAZIL only ! This indicator shows whether the products were produced inside Brazil or were imported. If the goods were imported, it also indicates whether they were imported directly by the company or not.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: Produced In-House
Use for BRAZIL only !
This indicator shows whether the product was produced by the company or another one. It is used for purpose of CFOP determination. If the indicator is set the product was produced by the company. If the indicator is not set the product was produced by another company.
Type: Text Length: 1

Product-Related Origin
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty. If this indicator is set, the product number will be written to the cost element itemization in the Controlling module.
Type: Text Length: 80

Indicator: With Qty Structure
This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Origin Group
Used for subdividing material and overhead costs.
When material costs are calculated, each material is given a general ledger account and thus is assigned to a primary cost element. If you enter an origin group for the material, the combination of source group and cost element is updated to the CO component. This means that you can Define calculation bases for determination of overhead rates for particular material groups. Define cost components in product costing for particular material groups. Create rules for activating work in process for each material group.
When overhead is calculated, an object is debited with overhead costs and another object (usually a cost center) is credited. For more information on the origin of overhead rates in the credit record, specify an origin group in the credit key. The system then displays the origin group in Cost Center Accounting, as well as the overhead cost element.
Type: Text Length: 80

Overhead Group
Key that groups products to which the same overhead is applied.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Margin
The valuation margin is the difference in percent between the retail value and the cost value of a material in relation to the retail value. It is used to determine the cost value of a value-only material at retail if no stock exists at retail. Valuation margin = (total retail - total cost) / total retail x 100
Type: Number Length: 6 Decimal: 2

Indicator: Accounting
You can use this indicator to set the accounting view on valuation level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1

Indicator: Costing
You can use this indicator to set the costing view on valuation level active. For further information please refer to the migration object documentation. This is an indicator field. If the criterion is met, enter X. If not, leave the field empty.
Type: Text Length: 1
Source Data for Migration Object: Product
Version SAP S/4HANA 2025 - Standard Scope - 03.02.2026 © Copyright SAP SE. All rights reserved.

Product Number*
A key that uniquely identifies the product.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Area*
Valuation area is the organizational level at which the product is valuated.
Type: Text Length: 80

Valuation Type
This is a further subdivision of the valuation category. The valuation category defines the valuation types allowed for a product. If a product is valuated by origin (for example, valuation category H), you can define the possible countries of origin as valuation types. For valuation category 'Single batch Valuation' you have to provide for each batch an own valuation type with the detailed price information. Note: you have to define one valuation header record with specified valuation category and initial valuation type and one dependent record for each valuation type. Further information can be found in the migration object documentation under Mapping instructions - Single Batch Valuation and Split Valuation.
Type: Text Length: 10

Valuation Category
Determines whether stocks of the material are valuated together or separately. In the case of split valuation, this indicator also determines which valuation types are allowed, that is, the criteria by which stocks can be valuated. Further information can be found the migration object documentation under Mapping instructions - Single Batch Valuation and Split Valuation.
Type: Text Length: 80

Currency Type*
The currency type specifies the role of a currency in the system. In addition to the local currency (company code currency), you can use parallel currencies per company code.
Type: Text Length: 80

Price Control*
Indicates the price control used to valuate the stock of a product. You have the following options: • S - Standard price • V - Moving average price
Type: Text Length: 80

Currency*
You must specify the currency belonging to the provided currency type.
Type: Text Length: 80

Inventory Price Moving Average
Moving average price if the product has price control indicator 'V' for moving average price. The system calculates the moving average price automatically by dividing the product value in the stock account by the total of all storage location stocks in the plant concerned. It changes the price with each valuation-relevant movement.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Inventory Price Standard Price
Standard price of the product if the product has price control indicator 'S' for standard price. The valuation of product stocks at standard prices means that all goods movements are valuated at the same price over an extended period.
Type: Number Length: 13 Decimal: 2 (the actual number of permitted decimals depends on the currency you entered with this amount)

Price Unit
Number of units to which the price refers.
Type: Number Length: 5
